blocks.text:
# SPRING GLOW (SGX) PROGRAM — CROSS-DEPARTMENTAL SPEND & RISK AUDIT MEMO

**Scope note on methodology:** This audit pulled every document in the knowledge base referencing "Spring Glow" or "SGX" across Campaign, Finance, Legal, HR, Operations, and IT records. A structural finding up front: **no single department holds a complete, unified ledger.** Each department (and in several cases, each document *version*) has a different partial view of the program, and several documents exist in duplicate "_v1" forms with subtle but material differences from the original. These are flagged throughout.

---

## 1. TOTAL PROGRAM COST — ALL CANDIDATE FIGURES AND RECONCILIATION

| # | Figure | Source Document | Department Owner | Scope |
|---|--------|-----------------|-------------------|-------|
| 1 | **$54,200** | `spring_glow_budget.csv` (file 343) — media 25,000 + production 15,000 + agency 8,000 + talent 5,000 + printing 1,200 | Campaign | Creative spend **only**; no vendor names, no staffing/IT/logistics/legal |
| 2 | **$435,000** | `budget_1720_421.txt` / `budget_1720_421_v1.md` (files 364, 350) — explicitly labeled **"Total Budget Allocated"**; prepared by Sarah Chen, 2026-01-28, referencing a vendor-contract review dated 2025-12-10 | Campaign/Finance | Cross-departmental (salaries 121,000; content creation 60,000; production/Melbournescope Studios 70,000; digital advertising 60,000; creative services 45,000; on-site activation + contingency ≈79,000) |
| 3 | **≥$283,333** (partial) | `budget_1300_924.md`/`.csv` (files 345, 1002); v1 `.csv` (352) | Finance/Technology/Staffing | Only 3 of an indicated larger set of line items visible: SouthCoast Logistics "Campaign Production" 158,333 (v1 shows **158,334** — $1 discrepancy), Southern Logistics "Event Coordination" 80,000, CRM Integration 45,000. Document summary references additional "analytics tools" and "creative-team staffing" rows not captured — true total is understated here. |
| 4 | **$534,833** (computed) | `budget_1750_589.md`/`.csv`/v1 (files 346, 1181, 351) | Marketing/Operations/Technology/Personnel | Skyline Analytics "Data Analytics" 158,333 (Approved) + EcoPackaging "Sustainable Packaging" 8,500 + Campaign Material Distribution 15,000 + CRM System Upgrade 48,000 + Department Staff Salaries 210,000 + Advertising 95,000. Several lines flagged **"Pending approval"** per document. |
| 5 | **≥$225,000** (partial) | `budget_1815_382.md` (file 1143) | Marketing/Legal-adjacent | Red Centre Legal "Campaign Advertising" 150,000 + EcoPackaging Australia "Social me[e]dia Management" 75,000 (typo in source). Vendor/category assignments look mismatched (see §3). |
| 6 | **$28,100** (itemized invoices, likely sub-component of #2/#4) | `invoice_0203_288`/v1 (files 260, 495): Red Centre Legal $8,450 + Dingo Consulting $7,200 = $15,650; `invoice_0091_116`/v1 (files 491, 490): NorthPac Legal $12,450 (legal review + IT infrastructure combined) | Campaign-authored, Legal/IT vendors | Actual invoiced legal/IT costs |
| 7 | **$45,000 minimum** | `memo_0568_213`/v1 (files 793, 812) — legal retainer, AUD 7,500/month × 6-month minimum term | Legal | Signed retainer, still under compliance re-review |

**Authoritative figure: $435,000 (file 364 / file 350).**
Reasoning:
- It is the **only document that explicitly declares itself a "Total Budget Allocated"** figure, rather than a raw list of line items an auditor must sum.
- It carries a named preparer (Sarah Chen), a preparation date, and an explicit reference to the vendor-contract review that grounds it.
- It is genuinely cross-departmental (staffing/salaries, production, content, digital, contingency) rather than a single-function subset.
- The competing spreadsheets (#3, #4, #5) show internal quality defects that undermine their reliability as "the" total: recurring identical dollar amounts ($158,333/$158,334) re-attributed to *different* vendors in different files (SouthCoast Logistics vs. Skyline Analytics — see §3), "Pending approval" statuses mixed in with approved lines, a data-entry typo ("meeldia"), and vendor/service-category mismatches (a legal firm billed under "Campaign Advertising"). These read like copy/paste template artifacts rather than an audited ledger.
- The $54,200 figure (#1) is explicitly narrow (creative line items only, no vendors named at all) and was never intended as a program total.

**Conclusion:** Total program cost = **$435,000 AUD** (Campaign/Finance total-budget record, file 364/350), with the caveat that itemized Legal/IT invoices (~$28,100, file 260/495/491/490) and the signed Legal retainer ($45,000 minimum, file 793/812) should be confirmed as either already included in that $435,000 envelope or as unbudgeted overage — the source documents do not make this traceable, which is itself a finding (see Recommendations).

---

## 2. RISK REGISTER

| Item | Department | Amount | Status | Source | Flag |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Red Centre Legal + Dingo Consulting invoice | Campaign (billing Legal-category vendors) | $15,650 ($8,450 + $7,200) | **Due/unpaid** | `invoice_0203_288.md` / `_v1.txt` (260/495) | Invoice number and year differ between the two stored copies (NMS-INV-**2026**-05-07-012 vs NMS-INV-**2027**-05-07-012) |
| NorthPac Legal invoice (legal review + IT infra bundled) | Campaign (billing Legal+IT vendor) | $12,450 | **Due/unpaid** | `invoice_0091_116.md` / `_v1.txt` (491/490) | Same year-shift defect: document date 2026-06-05 vs 2027-06-05 |
| Legal Advisory retainer (IP/brand protection/compliance) | Legal | $7,500/month, $45,000 min. | **Signed but under active compliance re-review** | `memo_0568_213` / `_v1` (793/812) | Author Freya Walsh; dated 2025-08-02 vs 2026-08-02 across copies |
| Legal Advisory Engagement M-77 chronology — discovery responses | Legal | N/A (litigation-support cost, not separately quantified) | **Open/unresolved** — discovery responses due 2026-02-28, retainer only signed 2026-01-10 | `chronology_M77.csv` (928) | Ongoing legal matter tied to the same engagement supporting SGX |
| Department Staff Salaries ($210,000) & Advertising ($95,000) lines | Finance/Operations/Personnel | $305,000 combined | **Pending approval** (per document) | `budget_1750_589.md`/csv (346/1181/351) | Nearly 57% of this ledger's $534,833 computed total is unapproved |
| SouthCoast Logistics "Campaign Production" | Finance/Technology/Staffing | $158,333 vs $158,334 (v1) | Approved (implied) but **inconsistent between copies** | `budget_1300_924.md`/csv (345/1002) vs v1 `.csv` (352) | $1 rounding/data-entry discrepancy |
| Red Centre Legal billed under "Campaign Advertising" | Legal/Marketing hybrid ledger | $150,000 | Not clearly approved/paid — no invoice found matching this amount anywhere else | `budget_1815_382.md` (1143) | Vendor/category mismatch — a legal firm billed as an advertising line (possible mis-booking) |
| EcoPackaging Australia billed under "Social me[e]dia Management" | Same ledger | $75,000 | Same as above | `budget_1815_382.md` (1143) | Packaging vendor billed for social media work — category mismatch |
| TVC packshot color-gradient discrepancy (Skyline Analytics render vs. approved design brief) | Campaign/IT | Not quantified — active production hold | **Unresolved / holding finalization** | `memo_0764_205.txt` / `_v1.txt` (727/401) | Version dates differ, 2025 vs 2026 |
| HiringMinds HR & Queensland Freight Co. compliance review (employee classification, safety regs) | Legal/HR/Operations | Not quantified — **no invoice or budget line found in Finance/Campaign ledgers** | Compliance review "in progress" | `email_1902_107` (643/1969), `email_1278_224` (657) | Cost of this vendor engagement is untraceable — see §3 |
| Email — Legal Advisory Engagement, subject mismatch | Legal | N/A | Data-integrity flag | `email_1902_107.eml` (1969, subject "M-77") vs `email_1902_107_v1.eml` (865, subject "M-78") — **identical sender (Zoe Patel), recipient (Ivy Kowalski), and timestamp (2025-08-13 05:30:00 UTC), but different engagement number** | Strongest fraud/template-reuse flag found in this audit |

---

## 3. VENDOR CROSS-REFERENCE

**Critical structural gap:** Campaign's own primary ledger (`spring_glow_budget.csv`, file 343) names **no vendors at all** — only abstract categories (media, production, agency, talent, printing). This makes a literal "not on Campaign's approved vendor list" check impossible from that document alone; I used the more detailed `budget_1720_421`/v1 (364/350), which does name vendors (e.g., Melbournescope Studios), as the best available proxy for "Campaign's own list." This gap is itself a finding: **Campaign has no vendor-level record**, while every other department's spreadsheet does.

| Vendor | Appears In (Dept/Doc) | Amount(s) Billed | Conflict? |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Red Centre Legal** | Campaign invoice `invoice_0203_288` ($8,450, legal review/compliance) vs. Legal-adjacent ledger `budget_1815_382` ($150,000, tagged "Campaign Advertising") | $8,450 vs. $150,000 | **YES — major conflict.** Same vendor, ~18x difference, and mismatched service category (legal firm billed as an ad vendor) |
| **EcoPackaging Australia** | `budget_1750_589` ($8,500, "Sustainable Packaging," Approved) vs. `budget_1815_382` ($75,000, "Social Media Management") | $8,500 vs. $75,000 | **YES — major conflict**, plus category mismatch (packaging vendor billed for social media) |
| **SouthCoast Logistics** | `budget_1300_924`/csv ($158,333) vs. v1 ($158,334) | $158,333 vs. $158,334 | Minor ($1) but present. Also **not listed** in Campaign's proxy vendor doc (364/350) — appears only in Finance/Technology/Staffing ledger |
| **Southern Logistics Pty Ltd** | `budget_1300_924` ($80,000, Event Coordination) | — | **Not in Campaign's own budget** (343 or 364/350) — flagged as an Operations vendor invisible to Campaign |
| **Skyline Analytics** | `budget_1750_589` ($158,333, Data Analytics, Approved); also named as TVC packshot vendor in `memo_0764_205` (Campaign/IT dispute) | $158,333 | Same dollar figure ($158,333) recurs for SouthCoast Logistics under a *different* category in a *different* file — likely a duplicated/templated number rather than two real, distinct $158,333 costs |
| **HiringMinds HR** | Legal correspondence only (643, 657, 1969/865) | **No dollar figure anywhere** | **Not in Campaign's vendor list, not in any Finance budget** — untraceable cost |
| **Queensland Freight Co.** | Legal correspondence (643, 657) and Operations status report (83) | **No invoice/budget line found** | **Not in Campaign's vendor list or Finance ledgers** |
| **Tasmanian IT** | Legal email 657 (data encryption) and vendor review (101/1464) | **No SGX-specific dollar figure** | **Not in Campaign or Finance SGX ledgers** |
| **TechFlow IT** | `memo_0764_205` (rendering investigation) and Legal email 859 (IP/data-handling review) | **No dollar figure** | **Not in Campaign or Finance SGX ledgers** |
| **NorthPac Legal** | Campaign invoice `invoice_0091_116` ($12,450, combined legal+IT) | $12,450 | Not in Campaign's proxy vendor doc; also bills unrelated projects (PPE Compliance Initiative, file 1686) at different rates — multi-project vendor, no SGX conflict but worth noting scope creep |
| **Dingo Consulting** | Campaign invoice `invoice_0203_288` ($7,200, market research) | $7,200 | Not listed in Campaign's own budget doc (364/350) |
| **Melbournescope Studios** | `budget_1720_421`/v1 ($70,000, Production) and email_0795_683 (spend consolidation request) | $70,000 | Consistent within Campaign's own docs — no conflict found |

**Headline vendor flags:** Red Centre Legal and EcoPackaging Australia both show **large, unreconciled billing conflicts and vendor/service mismatches** between the Finance/Legal-adjacent ledger (`budget_1815_382`) and other departmental records — these are the top two items for forensic follow-up.

---

## 4. RECONCILED PROGRAM-TOTAL VARIANCE

**All candidate totals found (repeated from §1 for clarity):**
1. $54,200 — `spring_glow_budget.csv` (343)
2. $435,000 — `budget_1720_421`/v1 (364/350) — *explicitly labeled total*
3. ≥$283,333 (partial) — `budget_1300_924` family (345/1002/352)
4. $534,833 (computed) — `budget_1750_589` family (346/1181/351)
5. ≥$225,000 (partial) — `budget_1815_382` (1143)

**Highest figure:** $534,833 (`budget_1750_589`, Operations/Technology/Personnel ledger)
**Lowest figure:** $54,200 (`spring_glow_budget.csv`, Campaign creative-only ledger)

**Variance = $534,833 − $54,200 = $480,633**
**As a percentage of the highest figure = $480,633 / $534,833 = 89.9%**

**Caveat on comparability:** This 89.9% gap is driven largely by scope mismatch — the low end is a 5-line creative-only sheet, the high end is a cross-functional (and partly unapproved) ledger. Using the two documents that are *actually* trying to state a comprehensive program total — the explicit $435,000 (`budget_1720_421`) versus the computed $534,833 (`budget_1750_589`) — narrows the "real" reconciliation gap to **$99,833, or 18.7% of the higher figure**. I recommend Finance treat **$99,833 (18.7%)** as the operative variance requiring resolution, while still disclosing the full $480,633/89.9% spread as evidence of how ungoverned the underlying spreadsheets are.

---

## 5. PERSON CROSS-REFERENCE (Signers/Senders/Approvers)

| Name | Title/Dept (as stated) | Document(s) | Flag |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Isla Carmichael** | Brand Strategist, Campaign | `invoice_0203_288` / v1 (260/495) | Title consistent across both copies, but invoice number/year differs (2026 vs 2027) between the original and "_v1" — pasted-template signature risk |
| **Marco Nguyen** | Campaign Manager, Campaign | `invoice_0091_116` / v1 (491/490) | Same pattern: identical title, but document date differs by exactly one year (2026-06-05 vs 2027-06-05) |
| **Paulo Eriksen** | Campaign Manager, Campaign | `project_update_1696_518` (701) | No conflicting copy found, but note below |
| **Freya Patel** | Campaign Manager (implied), Campaign | `email_0795_683` (470) | **Three different individuals (Nguyen, Eriksen, Patel) all hold the "Campaign Manager" title in the same department within the same ~6-week window (May–June 2026)** — plausible if multiple campaign managers exist, but warrants HR headcount confirmation given the other duplication issues found |
| **Sarah Chen** | Preparer (no title given), `budget_1720_421` total-budget document (364/350) | — | No title, no contact info, no cross-check possible — a documentation gap on the single most authoritative financial document in this audit |
| **Zoe Silva** | Legal Counsel, Legal | `status_report_1872_576` (79) | Dated 2025-11-10 |
| **Diego Okafor** | Paralegal, Legal | `status_report_0942_982` (94) | Dated 2025-09-05 |
| **Freya Walsh** | Paralegal, Legal | `memo_0568_213` / v1 (793/812) | Same content/title, but date shifts a full year between copies (2025-08-02 vs 2026-08-02) — consistent with the systemic "_v1 = +1 year" defect seen across this dataset |
| **Zoe Patel** | Sender (no title given) | `email_1902_107.eml` (1969) vs `email_1902_107_v1.eml` (865) | **Highest-severity flag:** identical sender, identical recipient (Ivy Kowalski), identical timestamp (2025-08-13 05:30:00 UTC) — but the subject line/engagement reference changes from "M-77" to "M-78." This is not a benign version artifact; it changes which legal matter the email is about while keeping every other metadata field frozen — classic sign of a copy-pasted/templated document rather than an independently authored one |
| **Ivy Kowalski** | Recipient, Legal (implied) | Same as above | No title or independent record found |

**Overall integrity observation:** A recurring pattern across this entire document set is that "_v1" copies preserve the author, title, and body text of the original but silently shift the **year by +1** (invoices 260/495, 491/490; memo 793/812) or, in one case, silently change the **subject-matter reference** while freezing every other field (1969/865). Individually these could be clerical version-control errors; taken together across Campaign, Legal, and Finance documents, they constitute a systemic data-integrity pattern that should be escalated — either a broken document-versioning process, or evidence of templated/fabricated records being inserted into the record set.

---

## 6. SOURCE CITATION INDEX
- Campaign creative budget: `spring_glow_budget.csv` (file 343)
- Total program budget ($435,000): `budget_1720_421.txt` (364), `budget_1720_421_v1.md` (350)
- Finance/Tech/Staffing ledger: `budget_1300_924.md` (345), `budget_1300_924.csv` (1002), `budget_1300_924_v1.csv` (352)
- Operations/Technology/Personnel ledger: `budget_1750_589.md` (346), `budget_1750_589.csv` (1181), `budget_1750_589_v1.csv` (351)
- Legal-adjacent ledger: `budget_1815_382.md` (1143)
- Legal/IT invoices: `invoice_0203_288.md`/`_v1.txt` (260/495), `invoice_0091_116.md`/`_v1.txt` (491/490)
- Legal retainer: `memo_0568_213.md`/`_v1.txt` (793/812)
- Legal chronology: `chronology_M77.csv` (928)
- Legal engagement correspondence: `email_1902_107.txt`/`.eml`/`_v1.eml` (643/1969/865), `email_1278_224.txt` (657), `email_1116_949.txt` (859)
- Campaign spend-consolidation request: `email_0795_683.txt` (470)
- Campaign project update: `project_update_1696_518.md` (701)
- Weekly legal status reports: `status_report_1872_576.txt` (79), `status_report_0942_982.md` (94)
- TVC/production hold: `memo_0764_205.txt`/`_v1.txt` (727/401)
- Vendor performance reviews: `vendor_review_1935_522.md` (134), `vendor_review_1131_348.md` (332), `vendor_review_1593_654.txt`/`_v1.txt` (101/1464), `vendor_review_1359_651.md` (726)

**Recommendation:** Freeze all "_v1" documents from further circulation until Legal/Finance can confirm which copy (if either) is the authentic original; require Campaign to produce a vendor-level ledger (it currently has none); and open a targeted investigation into the Red Centre Legal and EcoPackaging Australia billing conflicts before releasing further payment against the $28,100 in currently outstanding invoices.
